content:
- Finished story-09 end to end: marked the task and plan complete, ran `.ralph/task_switch.sh`, committed the verify-wrapper work as `fb9086f`, and pushed `master` successfully.
- Final verified lanes at completion: `make check`, `make lint`, `make test`, and `make test-long` all passed after the last `PathBuf` boundary cleanup in `molt_verify`.
- Remaining workspace note for the next agent: there is still an unrelated unstaged deletion at `.agents/skills/info-about-repo/SKILL.md` that I did not stage or revert.

- Read the saved progress log, refreshed the Ralph summaries, and applied the choose-task rules against `.ralph/current_tasks.md`, `.ralph/current_tasks_done.md`, and `.ralph/ralph-choose-task.md`.
- Found no active unblocked failing bug tasks, no visible `<blocked_by>` or `<priority>` tags in the refreshed active summary, and confirmed the repo is an actual Rust workspace (`Cargo.toml`, `crates/runner`, `crates/source-bootstrap`, `crates/ingest-contract`) rather than a copied template identity.
- Selected the next task as story 09 task 02 because story 09 task 01 already passes, story 09 remains the nearest unfinished verification/cutover chain, and it is the earliest unblocked failing task before the broader e2e and reporting stories. If quitting now, the next agent should read `.ralph/current_task.txt` and start `.ralph/tasks/story-09-verification-cutover/02-task-build-drain-to-zero-and-cutover-readiness-check.md` with TDD.

- Read saved progress and confirmed the next assigned work is `.ralph/tasks/story-09-verification-cutover/02-task-build-drain-to-zero-and-cutover-readiness-check.md`.
- Re-read the required `improve-code-boundaries` and `tdd` skill instructions so this turn follows the repo's mandated planning and TDD workflow.
- Next: read the task file, inspect any associated plan file, and determine whether the task is still in planning (`TO BE VERIFIED`) or ready for execution (`NOW EXECUTE`).

- Confirmed story 09 task 02 has no existing plan pointer, so this turn is a planning pass rather than implementation.
- Read the completed story 09 task 01 plan to match the expected local plan structure, checkbox style, and task-file `<plan>` linkage.
- Next: inspect current cutover/readiness-related design docs and runtime code to write a concrete execution plan with explicit TDD slices and boundary cleanup targets.

- Read the relevant cutover design docs and confirmed the readiness decision should combine three facts: received vs reconciled watermarks, per-table drain completion, and successful MOLT verification.
- Inspected the current runner runtime, tracking-state writes, and verify module; the key missing boundary is a read-side readiness module that owns SQL snapshot queries plus verify-artifact parsing instead of leaking those concerns into `lib.rs`.
- Next: finalize the command/interface choice, then write the task plan file and link it from the task before stopping as instructed.

- Wrote the plan for story 09 task 02 at `.ralph/tasks/story-09-verification-cutover/02-task-build-drain-to-zero-and-cutover-readiness-check_plans/2026-04-19-drain-to-zero-cutover-readiness-plan.md` and linked it from the task file.
- The plan is marked `NOW EXECUTE`; it chooses a new `runner cutover-readiness` command that queries live drain state, then runs fresh MOLT verification only after drain checks pass, keeping the readiness boundary in its own module.
- Stopped immediately after planning as required. Next agent should read the plan, execute the TDD slices in order, and only mark task acceptance boxes after `make check`, `make lint`, `make test`, and `make test-long` all pass.

- Re-read saved Ralph progress, current task pointer, and the required `tdd` plus `improve-code-boundaries` skills.
- Confirmed the assigned work is story 09 task 02 and prior context says its plan is already marked `NOW EXECUTE`, so this turn should execute the plan rather than re-plan.
- Next: read the task file and linked plan in full, inspect workspace state, then implement the plan as TDD slices and keep the progress log updated during each step.

- Read the story 09 task file and its linked plan. The plan ends with `NOW EXECUTE`, so the turn is in implementation mode.
- Captured current worktree state before touching code; there are unrelated pre-existing Ralph bookkeeping edits and one unrelated deleted skill file that should not be reverted.
- Next: inspect the runner CLI, tracking-state access, verify boundary, and existing integration test fixtures so the first TDD slice can be added as a real failing contract test.

- Inspected the current runner CLI, error types, tracking-state writes, and the existing MOLT verify boundary.
- Confirmed the main missing capability is a read-side readiness module: current code persists received/reconciled/table watermarks, but nothing reads and reduces them into an operator verdict.
- Next: read bootstrap/test helpers for the real helper-schema shape, then add the first failing cutover-readiness contract test against a live temporary Postgres plus fake `molt` executable.

- Added a new `runner cutover-readiness` command behind a dedicated `cutover_readiness` module plus typed `RunnerCutoverReadinessError` variants, keeping SQL/verdict logic out of `lib.rs`.
- Completed three red-green slices through the public CLI: ready=true happy path, watermark lag that skips verify, and table-level reconcile error that skips verify. The main fixes after the initial implementation were to tighten operator-facing reasons into stable phrases from the plan.
- Next: add the verify-mismatch not-ready contract, then cover loud missing-tracking-state failures, CLI help, and finally run the full required lanes with a last improve-code-boundaries pass.

- Finished the remaining targeted TDD slices: verify mismatches now keep `ready=false` with explicit mismatch wording, missing selected tracking rows fail loudly with helper-bootstrap diagnosis, and the new subcommand help now documents the operator contract.
- The new `cutover_readiness_contract` suite passes end to end, and `cli_contract` now exercises both root help listing and the descriptive `cutover-readiness --help` surface.
- Next: run the required repo lanes (`make check`, `make lint`, `make test`, `make test-long`), fix anything they surface, then do one final improve-code-boundaries pass and update Ralph/task bookkeeping only if every lane is green.
